note: GW38 final gameweek. Fixtures and ratings updated with confirmed news: Haaland unavailable, Gabriel out for season, Raya not in squad, Gvardiol benched, Sels auto-subs in.

fixture_ratings.notes: Confirmed NOT in Arsenal squad. Guaranteed zero. Sels auto-subs in as active GK replacement.
OUT for season — hamstring surgery confirmed. Guaranteed zero regardless of fixture quality. Status DOUBTFUL in cache is outdated; treat as DNP.
Confirmed benched. City are rotating — season position secured, Haaland also absent. Gvardiol may not even feature as a sub. Non-appearance risk very high despite easy FDR 2 opposition.

Forest vs Bournemouth at home. Dead rubber for both sides — neither chasing European spots or fighting relegation. Aina is a regular starter and will likely play 90. Clean sheet possible in low-stakes game but intensity is limited.

Liverpool likely champions so season motivation is muted for team. However Salah has personal records and Golden Boot incentives — near-certain starter. Away fixture adds difficulty. Vice-captain; safest armband alternative if Bowen underperforms.

Home fixture, FDR 2. Brentford mid-table with no existential stakes but Mbeumo is their key asset and will play. Home advantage helps. One of the cleanest starting spots in the squad — no rotation risk, good opponent.
Aston Villa home, FDR 2. European qualification may still be in play depending on final table — adds motivation relative to other dead rubbers. Rogers is a regular. Solid mid-price with upside.
Forest home vs Bournemouth. Same dead rubber caveat as Aina. Anderson contributes in buildup but has a limited goal/assist ceiling. Will likely start but returns may be modest.

CAPTAIN. WHU vs Leeds — West Ham in a relegation decider. This is a must-win context, meaning Bowen will be pushed forward with maximum intensity for the full 90. Home fixture. High ceiling. The one player in this squad with genuine maximum motivation in GW38. Best captaincy pick justified by stakes alone.
Away fixture, FDR 3. Crystal Palace mid-table with little at stake. Away game reduces clean sheet and scoring odds. Mateta will start but returns are hard to project in a low-motivation away trip.

Forest home vs Bournemouth. Wood is a physical, reliable starter. Dead rubber context means defensive intensity on both sides will be low — which can mean more open play and goal chances but less assured outcomes. Reasonable floor.

AUTO-SUBS IN as active GK replacing Raya. Forest home vs Bournemouth, FDR 2. Turns a guaranteed zero (Raya) into a live clean sheet chance. Forest at home in a calm match may keep things tight. Key auto-sub to confirm.
UNAVAILABLE — not in City squad for GW38. Guaranteed zero. FDR is irrelevant. Bench asset that cannot be utilised.
Bench DEF. Away fixture FDR 3. Palace have nothing to play for away from home. Clean sheet odds very low. Emergency cover only — not expected to contribute.
Bench MID. Bournemouth away at Forest — dead rubber both teams. Kluivert may feature but returns are unpredictable. Emergency cover only. Low priority.

best_fixtures: Bowen (WHU H vs Leeds) — relegation decider, maximum team and player motivation, home fixture, captain
Mbeumo (BRE H, FDR 2) — home, nailed starter, no rotation risk, clean opponent
Rogers (AVL H, FDR 2) — home, possible European spot motivation, reliable starter
Aina/Anderson/Wood (NFO H vs BOU, FDR 2) — home fixture, likely starters, though dead rubber caps ceiling
Sels (NFO H vs BOU) — auto-sub insurance converts Raya zero into a live clean sheet opportunity
avoid: Raya (ARS) — confirmed not in squad, guaranteed zero points
Gabriel (ARS) — out for season with hamstring surgery, guaranteed zero points
Haaland (MCI) — confirmed unavailable, guaranteed zero points
Gvardiol (MCI) — confirmed benched, Man City rotating entire squad with nothing at stake, non-appearance risk

high_motivation: WHU — relegation decider vs Leeds, Bowen is captain, maximum intensity expected
low_motivation_rotation_risk: MCI — season position secured, Haaland absent, Gvardiol benched, heavy rotation confirmed
ARS — Raya and Gabriel both out, squad rotation in progress
NFO vs BOU — mid-table dead rubber both sides, low intensity expected
LIV — likely champions, team motivation low though Salah has personal incentives

captain_analysis.verdict: Defensible. WHU vs Leeds in a relegation decider is the highest-motivation fixture in GW38. Home advantage. Bowen is West Ham's main creative threat and will play the full 90 in a must-win context. Risk: if WHU go behind early the game plan could change. High ceiling, medium floor.
captain_analysis.vc_verdict: Reasonable fallback. Salah has personal Golden Boot incentives even if Liverpool are champions and team rotation happens. Away fixture is the main concern. If Bowen blanks, Salah at least guarantees minutes.

dgw_bgw: none for GW38 — standard single gameweek. No double or blank gameweeks confirmed. All 20 teams play exactly one fixture. No TC chip opportunity (no DGW) and no FH trigger (no BGW).
